mnemonics.title: Handshaking Lemma
mnemonics.content: SUM of Degrees = 2 × EDGES. Think of handshaking: each handshake involves 2 people, total handshakes = (total hand-waves)/2.

mnemonics.title: Complete Graph Degree
mnemonics.content: In Kₙ, degree = n-1. Remember: you can't shake hands with yourself, so exclude yourself from the count.

mnemonics.title: Isolated vs Pendant
mnemonics.content: ISOlated = 0 (like ISOLATION = zero connections). PENDant = 1 (hanging by one thread).

acronyms.usage: Used to denote the largest vertex degree in graph G
Used to denote the smallest vertex degree in graph G
The set of all vertices in graph G
The set of all edges in graph G

visualTips.visualization: Draw the vertex as a dot and count all lines emanating from it. Each edge touching the vertex adds 1 to degree.
Imagine each edge as a handshake between two people (vertices). Count total hands involved = 2 per handshake.
Think of a square table where everyone has the same number of connections (legs touching).
Imagine a party where everyone shakes hands with everyone else - each person shakes (n-1) hands.

analogies.analogy: Think of vertices as people at a party and edges as handshakes. The degree is how many handshakes that person made.
An isolated vertex is like someone sitting alone in a corner - no one interacts with them (degree = 0).
A pendant vertex is like a person holding hands with only one other person at the party.
If 10 people each shake hands with 5 others, total handshakes is not 50 - it's 25 because each handshake involves 2 people.
